text.title: Spec Advanced Guidelines Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Spec Advanced Guidelines. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: When a trigger fires (multi-team / regulated / long-lived / source-of-truth) the matching block is mandatory.
rule.rationale: Optional blocks get skipped under deadline pressure.

rule.statement: NFR table specifies threshold + verification method for every NFR; 'fast enough' rejected.
Every AC scored against the rubric; AC below threshold rewritten.
Glossary terms are defined once in the spec; all other docs reference by ID.
Spec carries a versioning record (semver + last_reviewed); revisions append, never overwrite.
rule.rationale: Without revision history, reviewers cannot tell what changed.

rule.statement: If the spec is a source-of-truth, its content_id appears in the front-matter and downstream docs reference it.
rule.rationale: Without a stable pointer, downstream references drift.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-spec-advanced-guidelines.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
